551.iwh_en: It was narrated from a man among the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'After you — or behind you — will come the lying, misleading one (the Dajjal), and the back of his head will have curly ridges. He will say: I am your Lord. Whoever says: You have lied — you are not our Lord, but Allah is our Lord, in Him we trust and to Him we turn, and we seek refuge in Allah from you — that man will not be harmed by him and will not be put to trial by his rivers.'
551.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang laki-laki dari kalangan sahabat Nabi ﷺ bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Setelah kalian — atau di belakang kalian — akan datang sang pembohong yang menyesatkan (Dajjal), dan bagian belakang kepalanya terdapat rambut keriting berlapis-lapis. Ia akan berkata: Aku adalah Tuhanmu. Barang siapa berkata: Engkau telah berdusta — engkau bukan Tuhan kami, tetapi Allah adalah Tuhan kami, kepada-Nya kami bertawakal dan kepada-Nya kami kembali, dan kami berlindung kepada Allah darimu — maka orang itu tidak akan dicelakainya dan tidak akan tergoda oleh sungai-sungainya.'

552.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Burda that he said: I sat with an old man from the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ in the mosque of Kufa, and he narrated to me, saying: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ — or he said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said — 'O people, repent to Allah and seek His forgiveness, for I repent to Allah and seek His forgiveness one hundred times every day.'
552.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Burda bahwa ia berkata: Aku duduk bersama seorang syaikh dari kalangan sahabat Nabi ﷺ di masjid Kufah, lalu ia menceritakan kepadaku dan berkata: Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ — atau ia berkata: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda — 'Wahai manusia, bertaubatlah kepada Allah dan mohonlah ampunan-Nya, karena aku bertaubat kepada Allah dan memohon ampunan-Nya seratus kali setiap hari.'

553.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Nadra from one who heard the sermon of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in the middle days of Tashriq that he said: 'O people, indeed your Lord is one and your father is one. Know that no Arab has superiority over a non-Arab, nor does a non-Arab have superiority over an Arab, nor does a red person have superiority over a black person, nor a black person over a red person, except through taqwa. Have I conveyed the message?' They said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ has conveyed it.
553.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Nadra dari seseorang yang mendengar khutbah Rasulullah ﷺ pada hari-hari pertengahan Tasyriq bahwa beliau bersabda: 'Wahai manusia, sesungguhnya Tuhan kalian satu dan bapak kalian satu. Ketahuilah bahwa tidak ada kelebihan orang Arab atas orang non-Arab, tidak pula orang non-Arab atas orang Arab, tidak pula orang berkulit merah atas orang berkulit hitam, tidak pula orang berkulit hitam atas orang berkulit merah, kecuali dengan takwa. Sudahkah aku menyampaikan?' Mereka berkata: Rasulullah ﷺ telah menyampaikannya.'

554.iwh_en: It was narrated from Yazid ibn Abi Habib that Marthad ibn Abdillah never came to the mosque without bringing something to give in charity. One day he came to the mosque with an onion, and he said: O Abu al-Khayr, why do you do this — it will make your garment smell foul. He said: O nephew, by Allah, there was nothing in my house that I could give in charity other than this. Indeed, a man from the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ narrated to me that every act of charity is a shade for its giver on the Day of Resurrection.
554.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Yazid ibn Abi Habib bahwa Marthad ibn Abdillah tidak pernah datang ke masjid kecuali membawa sesuatu untuk disedekahkan. Suatu hari ia datang ke masjid membawa bawang merah, maka ia berkata kepadanya: Wahai Abu al-Khayr, mengapa kamu melakukan ini — ia akan membuat pakaianmu berbau busuk. Ia berkata: Wahai keponakanku, demi Allah, tidak ada di rumahku sesuatu yang bisa aku sedekahkan selain ini. Sesungguhnya seorang laki-laki dari kalangan sahabat Nabi ﷺ menceritakan kepadaku bahwa setiap amal sedekah merupakan naungan bagi pelakunya pada hari kiamat.'

555.iwh_en: It was narrated from Arfaja from a man among the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ that he mentioned Ramadan and said: 'In it the gates of Paradise are opened, the gates of Hell are closed, the devils are chained, and a caller calls out every night: O seeker of good, come forward! O seeker of evil, desist! — until Ramadan comes to an end.'
555.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Arfajah dari seorang laki-laki dari kalangan sahabat Nabi ﷺ bahwa ia menyebut Ramadan dan berkata: 'Di dalamnya pintu-pintu surga dibuka, pintu-pintu neraka ditutup, setan-setan dibelenggu, dan seorang penyeru berseru setiap malam: Wahai pencari kebaikan, kemarilah! Wahai pencari kejahatan, berhentilah! — hingga Ramadan berakhir.'

556.iwh_en: It was narrated from a Bedouin man that he said: I brought merchandise to Madinah during the lifetime of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. When I finished my trade, I said to myself: I will certainly meet this man and hear from him. So he met me between Abu Bakr and Umar as they were walking. I followed behind them until they came upon a Jewish man who had the Torah spread open and was reading it to console himself over the loss of a son. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to him: I ask you by the One Who revealed the Torah, do you find in it that I am the Prophet of Allah? The Jewish man looked at his companions and they were silent. Then he said: Yes, by the One Who revealed the Torah, we find in it the description of you and your coming, but we were afraid that if we followed you, the Arabs would expel us from our land.
556.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang laki-laki Badui bahwa ia berkata: Aku membawa barang dagangan ke Madinah semasa hidup Rasulullah ﷺ. Ketika selesai berdagang, aku berkata dalam hati: Sungguh aku akan menemui orang ini dan mendengar darinya. Maka beliau menemuiku di antara Abu Bakr dan Umar saat mereka berjalan. Aku mengikuti di belakang mereka hingga mereka mendatangi seorang Yahudi yang membentangkan Taurat dan membacanya untuk menghibur dirinya atas kehilangan seorang anak. Rasulullah ﷺ berkata kepadanya: Aku bertanya kepadamu demi Dzat yang menurunkan Taurat, apakah kamu menemuiku dalam Taurat sebagai Nabi Allah? Lelaki Yahudi itu memandang kepada para sahabatnya dan mereka diam. Kemudian ia berkata: Ya, demi Dzat yang menurunkan Taurat, kami menemukan di dalamnya deskripsimu dan kedatanganmu, namun kami khawatir jika kami mengikutimu maka orang Arab akan mengusir kami dari negeri kami.'

557.iwh_en: It was narrated from a man among the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ that he said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ delivered a sermon at the time of the Conquest — and in one narration: on the day of the Conquest of Makkah — and said: 'There is no god but Allah alone, He fulfilled His promise, supported His servant, and defeated the confederates alone. Know that every claim of honor, every blood claim, and every financial claim from the time of ignorance is now beneath these two feet of mine, except the custodianship of the Kaaba and the provision of water to pilgrims.'
557.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang laki-laki dari kalangan sahabat Nabi ﷺ bahwa ia berkata: Rasulullah ﷺ berkhutbah pada saat Fathu Makkah — dan dalam satu riwayat: pada hari Fathu Makkah — dan bersabda: 'Tidak ada ilah selain Allah semata, Dia menepati janji-Nya, menolong hamba-Nya, dan mengalahkan pasukan sekutu sendirian. Ketahuilah bahwa setiap kebanggaan, setiap tuntutan darah, dan setiap tuntutan harta dari masa jahiliyah kini berada di bawah kedua kakiku ini, kecuali pengawasan Kakbah dan pemberian air minum kepada jamaah haji.'

558.iwh_en: It was narrated from a man among the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever suffers an affliction in his body and forgoes it for the sake of Allah, it will be an expiation for him.'
558.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang laki-laki dari kalangan sahabat Nabi ﷺ bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Barang siapa ditimpa musibah pada tubuhnya lalu ia menanggungnya karena Allah, maka itu akan menjadi kafarat (penebus dosa) baginya.'

559.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ibrahim al-Ashhali from his father that he came to him and informed him — or told him — that he heard the Prophet ﷺ say in the funeral prayer: 'O Allah, forgive our living and our dead, those present and those absent, our males and our females, our young and our old.'
559.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ibrahim al-Asyhali dari ayahnya bahwa ia mendatanginya dan memberitahunya — atau mengabarkannya — bahwa ia mendengar Nabi ﷺ bersabda dalam shalat jenazah: 'Ya Allah, ampunilah orang-orang kami yang masih hidup dan yang telah meninggal, yang hadir dan yang tidak hadir, laki-laki dan perempuan kami, yang muda dan yang tua kami.'

560.iwh_en: It was narrated from a man of his people that he heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say three times: 'Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him honor his guest. Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him do good to his neighbor. Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him speak good or remain silent.'
560.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang laki-laki dari kaumnya bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda tiga kali: 'Barang siapa beriman kepada Allah dan hari akhir, hendaklah ia memuliakan tamunya. Barang siapa beriman kepada Allah dan hari akhir, hendaklah ia berbuat baik kepada tetangganya. Barang siapa beriman kepada Allah dan hari akhir, hendaklah ia berkata baik atau diam.'

561.iwh_en: It was narrated from a man among the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ that he said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood among us on a red she-camel that had part of her ear cut off and said: 'Do you know what day this is?' We said: The Day of Sacrifice. He said: 'You are correct — the day of the greater pilgrimage. Do you know what month this is?' We said: Dhu al-Hijja. He said: 'You are correct. Do you know what land this is?' We said: The Sacred Land. He said: 'Indeed, your blood, your wealth, and your honor are sacred to one another, just as the sanctity of this day in this month in this land, until you meet your Lord. Have I conveyed the message?'
561.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang laki-laki dari kalangan sahabat Nabi ﷺ bahwa ia berkata: Rasulullah ﷺ berdiri di tengah kami di atas unta merah yang dipotong telinganya dan bersabda: 'Apakah kalian tahu hari apakah ini?' Kami menjawab: Hari Nahr (kurban). Beliau bersabda: 'Benar, hari Haji Akbar. Apakah kalian tahu bulan apakah ini?' Kami menjawab: Dzulhijjah. Beliau bersabda: 'Benar. Apakah kalian tahu negeri apakah ini?' Kami menjawab: Negeri Haram. Beliau bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya darah kalian, harta kalian, dan kehormatan kalian haram satu sama lain, sebagaimana haramnya hari ini di bulan ini di negeri ini, sampai kalian bertemu Tuhan kalian. Sudahkah aku menyampaikan?'

562.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: Advise me and be brief. He said: 'When you stand in your prayer, pray the prayer of a man bidding farewell. Do not say something for which you will have to make excuses tomorrow. And give up hope in what people possess.'
562.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: Nasihatilah aku dan ringkaslah. Beliau bersabda: 'Jika engkau berdiri dalam shalatmu, maka shalatlah seperti shalat orang yang berpamitan. Janganlah mengucapkan sesuatu yang harus kamu mintakan maaf esok hari. Dan lepaskanlah harapan dari apa yang ada di tangan manusia.'

563.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was in a naval expedition when they passed by the one in charge of dividing the captives, and a woman was weeping. He asked: What is the matter with this woman? They said: She was separated from her child. So he took the hand of her child and placed it in her hand.
563.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berada dalam suatu ekspedisi laut ketika mereka melewati petugas yang membagi tawanan, dan seorang wanita sedang menangis. Ia bertanya: Ada apa dengan wanita ini? Mereka berkata: Ia dipisahkan dari anaknya. Maka ia mengambil tangan anak itu dan meletakkannya di tangan sang ibu.

564.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'The great cities will be opened to you, and they will impose military expeditions upon you. A man among you may dislike the expedition and slip away from his people, offering himself to the tribes saying: Who will spare me from his expedition? That is not permissible. Let no man do that — I am a guarantor for the one who goes out in the path of Allah.'
564.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Kota-kota besar akan dibuka untuk kalian, dan mereka akan memberlakukan penugasan militer atas kalian. Seorang laki-laki di antara kalian mungkin tidak menyukai penugasan itu dan melepaskan diri dari kaumnya, menawarkan dirinya kepada kabilah-kabilah sambil berkata: Siapa yang akan membebaskanku dari penugasannya? Itu tidak diperbolehkan. Janganlah seorang pun melakukan itu — aku adalah penjamin bagi orang yang keluar di jalan Allah.'

565.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever comes worshipping Allah without associating anything with Him, establishes the prayer, pays the zakat, fasts Ramadan, and avoids the major sins — he will have Paradise.' They asked him: What are the major sins? He said: 'Associating partners with Allah, killing a Muslim soul, and fleeing on the day of battle.'
565.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barang siapa datang menyembah Allah tanpa menyekutukan-Nya dengan sesuatu pun, mendirikan shalat, membayar zakat, berpuasa Ramadan, dan menjauhi dosa-dosa besar — maka baginya surga.' Mereka bertanya kepadanya: Apa saja dosa-dosa besar itu? Beliau bersabda: 'Menyekutukan Allah, membunuh jiwa seorang Muslim, dan melarikan diri pada hari pertempuran.'

566.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ used to say: 'Indeed, every prayer wipes away the sins that preceded it.'
566.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya setiap shalat menghapus dosa-dosa yang dilakukan sebelumnya.'

567.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was brought a bowl containing onion, and he said: 'Eat,' but he himself declined to eat, saying: 'I am not like any of you.'
567.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: Rasulullah ﷺ dibawakan semangkuk berisi bawang, lalu beliau bersabda: 'Makanlah,' namun beliau sendiri menolak untuk makan, seraya bersabda: 'Aku tidak seperti salah seorang dari kalian.'

568.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came out to them one day and said: 'Indeed my Lord, Exalted and Glorified, gave me a choice between seventy thousand entering Paradise without reckoning, and a hidden treasure reserved with Him for my nation.' He said: I chose the hidden treasure for my nation.
568.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ keluar menemui mereka suatu hari dan bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya Tuhanku 'Azza wa Jalla memberiku pilihan antara tujuh puluh ribu orang yang masuk surga tanpa hisab, dan rahasia tersembunyi yang disimpan di sisi-Nya untuk umatku.' Beliau berkata: Aku memilih rahasia tersembunyi itu untuk umatku.'

569.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever worships Allah without associating anything with Him, establishes the prayer, pays the zakat, fasts Ramadan, and avoids the major sins — he will have Paradise, or he will enter Paradise.' He was asked: What are the major sins? He said: 'Associating partners with Allah, killing a Muslim soul, and fleeing on the day of battle.'
569.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barang siapa menyembah Allah tanpa menyekutukan-Nya dengan sesuatu pun, mendirikan shalat, membayar zakat, berpuasa Ramadan, dan menjauhi dosa-dosa besar — maka baginya surga, atau ia akan masuk surga.' Ditanyakan kepadanya: Apa saja dosa-dosa besar itu? Beliau bersabda: 'Menyekutukan Allah, membunuh jiwa seorang Muslim, dan melarikan diri pada hari pertempuran.'

570.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ arrived in Madinah, the Ansar drew lots as to which of them would host the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and Abu Ayyub won the draw and hosted the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Whenever food was gifted to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he would send the remainder to Abu Ayyub.
570.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika Rasulullah ﷺ tiba di Madinah, kaum Ansar mengundi siapa di antara mereka yang akan menampung Rasulullah ﷺ, dan Abu Ayyub menang dalam undian itu sehingga menampung Rasulullah ﷺ. Setiap kali makanan dihadiahkan kepada Rasulullah ﷺ, beliau mengirimkan sisanya kepada Abu Ayyub.'

571.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Measure your food and you will be blessed in it.'
571.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Takaruklah makananmu, niscaya kamu akan diberkahi di dalamnya.'

572.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Measure your food and you will be blessed in it.'
572.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Takaruklah makananmu, niscaya kamu akan diberkahi di dalamnya.'

573.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The hand of Allah is with the judge when he judges, and the hand of Allah is with the distributor when he divides.'
573.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tangan Allah bersama hakim ketika ia memutuskan perkara, dan tangan Allah bersama pembagi ketika ia membagi.'

574.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that when they debated about fermenting drinks in gourd vessels, he said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbid every pitched vessel used for fermenting drinks.
574.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika mereka berdebat tentang membuat minuman fermentasi dalam wadah labu, ia berkata: Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ melarang setiap wadah yang dilapisi tar yang digunakan untuk membuat minuman fermentasi.'

575.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever separates a child from its parent in a sale, Allah will separate him from his beloved ones on the Day of Resurrection.'
575.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barang siapa memisahkan seorang anak dari orang tuanya dalam suatu penjualan, Allah akan memisahkannya dari orang-orang yang dicintainya pada hari kiamat.'

576.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said while he was in Egypt: By Allah, I do not know how to handle these latrines, for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When one of you goes to relieve himself, let him not face the qibla and let him not turn his back to it.'
576.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata saat berada di Mesir: Demi Allah, aku tidak tahu bagaimana cara menggunakan kakus-kakus ini, karena Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jika salah seorang dari kalian pergi buang air besar atau kecil, janganlah menghadap kiblat dan jangan pula membelakanginya.'

577.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that when death approached him, he said: I used to conceal from you something I heard from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying: 'Were it not that you sin, Allah the Blessed and Exalted would create a people who sin, and then He would forgive them.'
577.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika ajal menghampirinya, ia berkata: Aku dulu menyembunyikan dari kalian sesuatu yang aku dengar dari Rasulullah ﷺ yang bersabda: 'Seandainya kalian tidak berdosa, Allah Yang Maha Berkah dan Maha Tinggi akan menciptakan suatu kaum yang berdosa, lalu Dia mengampuni mereka.'

578.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ arrived in Madinah, he stayed with him and said to him: 'O Abu Ayyub, shall I not teach you?' He said: I replied: Certainly, O Messenger of Allah. He said: 'No servant who says in the morning: There is no god but Allah alone, with no partner, to Him belongs the kingship and to Him belongs all praise, and He is over all things capable — but that ten good deeds are recorded for him, ten bad deeds are erased, he is raised ten degrees, and he is protected from the Devil until the evening.'
578.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika Rasulullah ﷺ tiba di Madinah, beliau tinggal bersamanya dan berkata kepadanya: 'Wahai Abu Ayyub, maukah kamu aku ajarkan?' Ia berkata: Aku menjawab: Tentu, wahai Rasulullah. Beliau bersabda: 'Tidaklah seorang hamba yang mengucapkan di pagi hari: Laa ilaaha illallaahu wahdahu laa syariika lahu, lahul mulku wa lahul hamdu wa huwa 'alaa kulli syai-in qadiir — kecuali dicatat baginya sepuluh kebaikan, dihapus darinya sepuluh keburukan, ia dinaikkan sepuluh derajat, dan ia terlindungi dari setan hingga sore hari.'

579.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ stayed at his house, and the Prophet ﷺ stayed on the lower floor while Abu Ayyub stayed on the upper floor. One night Abu Ayyub awoke and said: We are walking above the head of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. So they moved to the lower floor and the Prophet ﷺ moved to the upper floor, and the Prophet ﷺ was pleased with that.
579.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ singgah di rumahnya, lalu Nabi ﷺ tinggal di lantai bawah sementara Abu Ayyub tinggal di lantai atas. Suatu malam Abu Ayyub terbangun dan berkata: Kami berjalan di atas kepala Rasulullah ﷺ. Maka mereka pindah ke lantai bawah dan Nabi ﷺ pindah ke lantai atas, dan Nabi ﷺ senang dengan hal itu.'

580.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever says after the Fajr prayer: There is no god but Allah alone, with no partner, to Him belongs the kingship and to Him belongs all praise, and He is over all things capable — ten times — they will be like the reward of freeing ten slaves, ten good deeds will be recorded for him, ten bad deeds will be erased, and he will have the protection of Allah until the evening.'
580.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barang siapa mengucapkan setelah shalat Subuh: Laa ilaaha illallaahu wahdahu laa syariika lahu, lahul mulku wa lahul hamdu wa huwa 'alaa kulli syai-in qadiir — sepuluh kali — maka pahalanya seperti memerdekakan sepuluh budak, dicatat baginya sepuluh kebaikan, dihapus darinya sepuluh keburukan, dan ia mendapat perlindungan Allah hingga sore hari.'

581.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: We do not know how to handle the latrines of Egypt, for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade us to face either of the two qiblas or to turn our backs to them — meaning when relieving oneself.
581.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: Kami tidak tahu bagaimana cara menggunakan kakus-kakus Mesir, karena Rasulullah ﷺ melarang kami menghadap salah satu dari dua kiblat atau membelakanginya — yaitu saat buang air.'

582.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'There is no man who plants a tree but Allah records for him a reward equal to what comes forth from the fruits of that tree.'
582.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidaklah seorang laki-laki menanam pohon kecuali Allah mencatat baginya pahala sebesar apa yang dihasilkan dari buah pohon itu.'

583.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'Hasten to pray the Maghrib prayer before the star rises.'
583.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Bersegeralah melaksanakan shalat Maghrib sebelum bintang terbit.'

584.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: We were with the Prophet ﷺ one day and he served food. I never saw any food that was more blessed at the beginning of our eating and less blessed at the end. We said: How is this, O Messenger of Allah? He said: 'Because we mentioned the name of Allah when we began eating, but then someone joined who did not mention the name of Allah, so the Devil ate with him.'
584.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: Kami bersama Nabi ﷺ suatu hari dan beliau menyajikan makanan. Aku tidak pernah melihat makanan yang lebih berkah di awal kami makan namun kurang berkah di akhirnya. Kami berkata: Bagaimana ini, wahai Rasulullah? Beliau bersabda: 'Karena kami menyebut nama Allah ketika kami mulai makan, tetapi kemudian seseorang bergabung yang tidak menyebut nama Allah, sehingga setan makan bersamanya.'

585.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that when death was near, he said: Convey my greetings to the people and tell them that I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'Whoever dies without associating anything with Allah, Allah will place him in Paradise.' Then carry me forward and bury me deep in enemy territory.
585.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika ajal mendekat, ia berkata: Sampaikan salamku kepada orang-orang dan kabarkanlah kepada mereka bahwa aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barang siapa meninggal tanpa menyekutukan Allah dengan sesuatu pun, Allah akan menempatkannya di surga.' Kemudian bawalah aku maju dan kuburkanlah aku jauh di wilayah musuh.'

586.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When one of you goes to relieve himself, let him not face the qibla, but rather let him face east or west.' Abu Ayyub said: When we came to Syria, we found latrines built facing the qibla, so we would turn away and seek forgiveness from Allah.
586.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jika salah seorang dari kalian pergi buang hajat, janganlah ia menghadap kiblat, tetapi hendaklah menghadap ke timur atau ke barat.' Abu Ayyub berkata: Ketika kami tiba di Syam, kami menemukan kakus-kakus yang dibuat menghadap kiblat, maka kami berpaling dan memohon ampun kepada Allah.'

587.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was brought food, he would eat from it and then send the remainder to Abu Ayyub. One day he sent a bowl that contained garlic and the Prophet ﷺ had not eaten from it. Abu Ayyub asked: Is it forbidden? He said: 'No, but I dislike it because of its smell.' He said: Then I too dislike what you dislike.
587.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ dibawakan makanan, beliau memakannya lalu mengirimkan sisanya kepada Abu Ayyub. Suatu hari beliau mengirimkan semangkuk yang berisi bawang putih dan Nabi ﷺ tidak memakannya sama sekali. Abu Ayyub bertanya: Apakah itu haram? Beliau bersabda: 'Tidak, tetapi aku tidak menyukainya karena baunya.' Ia berkata: Maka aku pun tidak menyukai apa yang tidak kamu sukai.'

588.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that whenever the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was brought food, he would eat from it as much as Allah willed, then send the rest to Abu Ayyub with the mark of his hand on it. Once he was brought a dish containing garlic and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ ate nothing from it and sent it to Abu Ayyub. His family said to him: He says draw it near to you, for he dislikes it, but it is not forbidden.
588.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa setiap kali Rasulullah ﷺ dibawakan makanan, beliau memakannya sebanyak yang Allah kehendaki, kemudian mengirimkan sisanya kepada Abu Ayyub dengan bekas tangan beliau di situ. Suatu kali beliau dibawakan hidangan berisi bawang putih dan Rasulullah ﷺ tidak memakannya sama sekali dan mengirimkannya kepada Abu Ayyub. Keluarganya berkata kepadanya: Beliau berkata dekatkanlah ia kepadamu, karena beliau tidak menyukainya, namun itu tidak haram.'

589.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'How excellent are those who clean between the fingers and toes.' It was asked: What are those who clean between the fingers and toes? He said: 'In wudu and when eating.'
589.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Alangkah baiknya orang-orang yang membersihkan sela-sela jari.' Ditanyakan: Siapa orang-orang yang membersihkan sela-sela jari itu? Beliau bersabda: 'Dalam wudhu dan ketika makan.'

590.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) with mention of the Prophet ﷺ that he said: 'It is not permissible for a Muslim to shun his brother for more than three days — they meet and this one turns away and that one turns away. The better of them is the one who begins with the greeting of peace.'
590.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dengan menyebutkan Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda: 'Tidak halal bagi seorang Muslim untuk memutus hubungan dengan saudaranya lebih dari tiga hari — mereka bertemu dan yang satu berpaling dan yang lain berpaling. Yang terbaik di antara keduanya adalah yang memulai dengan salam.'

591.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that al-Miswar and Ibn Abbas disagreed — and in one narration: they disputed — about whether a person in the state of ihram may pour water on his head. They sent someone to ask Abu Ayyub: How did you see the Messenger of Allah ﷺ wash his head? He said: Like this — moving from front to back and from back to front, and Sufyan described it.
591.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa al-Miswar dan Ibnu Abbas berselisih — dan dalam satu riwayat: berdebat — tentang apakah orang yang berihram boleh menuangkan air di kepalanya. Mereka mengutus seseorang untuk bertanya kepada Abu Ayyub: Bagaimana kamu melihat Rasulullah ﷺ mencuci kepalanya? Ia berkata: Seperti ini — menggerakkan dari depan ke belakang dan dari belakang ke depan, dan Sufyan menggambarkannya.'

592.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The best charity is charity given to a hostile relative.'
592.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sebaik-baik sedekah adalah sedekah yang diberikan kepada kerabat yang memusuhi.'

593.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Water is from water.'
593.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Air (mandi wajib) disebabkan oleh air (keluarnya mani).'

594.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was persistent in praying four rakats at the time of the sun's decline from its zenith. I said: O Messenger of Allah, what are these rakats that I see you are persistent in performing? He said: 'The gates of heaven are opened at the time of the sun's decline from its zenith and they do not close until Zuhr is prayed. I love that a good deed of mine ascends to Allah during that time.'
594.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: Rasulullah ﷺ senantiasa melakukan empat rakaat saat matahari tergelincir dari puncaknya. Aku berkata: Wahai Rasulullah, apakah rakaat-rakaat ini yang aku lihat selalu engkau kerjakan? Beliau bersabda: 'Pintu-pintu langit dibuka saat matahari tergelincir dari puncaknya dan tidak ditutup hingga Zuhur dikerjakan. Aku suka agar amal baikku naik kepada Allah di waktu itu.'

595.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever fasts Ramadan and then follows it with six days of Shawwal, that is equivalent to fasting the entire year.'
595.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barang siapa berpuasa Ramadan kemudian mengikutinya dengan enam hari di bulan Syawal, maka itu setara dengan puasa setahun penuh.'

596.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that when he came on a military expedition and Uqba ibn Amir was the governor of Egypt at that time, and Uqba delayed the Maghrib prayer, Abu Ayyub stood up and said: What is this prayer, O Uqba? He said: We were occupied. He said: By Allah, I am only concerned that people will think you saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ do this. Did you not hear him say: 'My nation will always be in a good state — or he said: upon the fitrah — as long as they do not delay the Maghrib until the stars appear overlapping?'
596.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika ia datang dalam suatu ekspedisi militer dan Uqbah ibn Amir saat itu menjabat gubernur Mesir, Uqbah mengakhirkan shalat Maghrib, maka Abu Ayyub berdiri dan berkata: Shalat apa ini, wahai Uqbah? Ia berkata: Kami sibuk. Ia berkata: Demi Allah, aku hanya khawatir orang-orang mengira kamu melihat Rasulullah ﷺ melakukan ini. Tidakkah kamu mendengar beliau bersabda: 'Umatku akan senantiasa dalam keadaan baik — atau beliau bersabda: di atas fitrah — selama mereka tidak mengakhirkan Maghrib hingga bintang-bintang tampak bertumpuk?'

597.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When one of you goes to the toilet, let him not face the qibla and let him not turn his back to it — rather, face east or west.' Abu Ayyub said: When we came to Syria, we found seats that faced the qibla, so we would turn away from them and seek forgiveness from Allah the Exalted and Glorified.
597.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jika salah seorang dari kalian pergi ke toilet, janganlah menghadap kiblat dan jangan pula membelakanginya — melainkan menghadaplah ke timur atau ke barat.' Abu Ayyub berkata: Ketika kami tiba di Syam, kami menemukan tempat duduk yang menghadap kiblat, maka kami berpaling darinya dan memohon ampun kepada Allah 'Azza wa Jalla.'

598.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ ate food, he would send the remainder to Abu Ayyub. One day he sent a bowl that contained garlic, and Abu Ayyub said: O Messenger of Allah, is it forbidden? He said: 'No, but I dislike its smell.' He said: Then I too dislike what you dislike.
598.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ jika makan makanan, beliau mengirimkan sisanya kepada Abu Ayyub. Suatu hari beliau mengirimkan semangkuk yang berisi bawang putih. Maka Abu Ayyub berkata: Wahai Rasulullah, apakah itu haram? Beliau bersabda: 'Tidak, tetapi aku tidak menyukai baunya.' Ia berkata: Maka aku pun tidak menyukai apa yang kamu tidak sukai.'

599.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that a Bedouin man accosted the Prophet ﷺ while he was traveling and seized the nose-rope or bridle of his camel and said: O Messenger of Allah — or: O Muhammad — inform me of what will bring me closer to Paradise and farther from the Fire. He said: 'Worship Allah without associating anything with Him, establish the prayer, pay the zakat, and maintain ties of kinship.' Then he released the camel.
599.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki Badui menghadang Nabi ﷺ saat beliau sedang dalam perjalanan dan memegang tali hidung atau kendali untanya, lalu berkata: Wahai Rasulullah — atau: Wahai Muhammad — beritahukan kepadaku apa yang akan mendekatkanku ke surga dan menjauhkanku dari neraka. Beliau bersabda: 'Sembahlah Allah tanpa menyekutukan-Nya dengan sesuatu pun, dirikan shalat, bayar zakat, dan jalin silaturahmi.' Kemudian ia melepaskan unta itu.'

600.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ went out after the sun had set and heard a sound and said: 'Jews are being punished in their graves.'
600.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ keluar setelah matahari terbenam dan mendengar suara, lalu bersabda: 'Orang-orang Yahudi sedang disiksa di dalam kubur mereka.'
